title: Inside the Wire
learning_objectives: Assess compromised system for critical information
Discover and extract credentials from system configurations
Map internal network from compromised host
key_topics: Sensitive file identification and data discovery
scenario: Low-privilege shell on a Linux server. Perform comprehensive post-exploitation: enumeration, credential hunting, network recon, data discovery.

scenario_context.setting: Compromised Linux web server with limited privileges
scenario_context.challenge: Maximize intelligence extraction from limited access point
scenario_context.relevance: Linux is dominant in cloud/server infrastructure; essential skill

subtasks.title: Situational Assessment
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Run: id, whoami, uname -a, hostname, ifconfig, ps aux, sudo -l. Document current user permissions and system configuration.

subtasks.title: Automated Enumeration
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Execute LinPEAS script on target. Parse output. Identify top privilege escalation candidates, misconfigurations, and critical findings.

subtasks.title: Credential Hunting
subtasks.description: History, config files, SSH keys, DB strings.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Search shell history, .bashrc, .profile, /etc/config files for credentials. Find SSH keys, DB connection strings. Document all credentials found.

subtasks.title: Network Reconnaissance
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Run: netstat -tlnp, ss -tlnp, arp -a, route, traceroute. Identify internal networks and accessible systems from this host.
subtasks.evidence_required: Internal network map from compromised host perspective

subtasks.title: Data Discovery
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Search for sensitive data: /home/*, /var/backups, /opt, database files, source code repositories. Create inventory.
subtasks.evidence_required: Sensitive data inventory with risk assessment

subtasks.title: Post-Exploitation Report
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Comprehensive report summarizing: system assessment, findings, credentials discovered, network topology, accessible data, and lateral movement opportunities.

reflection_prompt: What hidden credentials did you discover and how would they enable further compromise? What network topology did you uncover?

quiz.question: What is the primary value of examining shell history files (.bash_history)?
options: Sees executed commands including potential credentials
quiz.explanation: Shell history often contains credentials passed as command arguments, which is a critical discovery vector.

quiz.question: How can SSH keys discovered during post-exploitation enable lateral movement?
options: They're only useful for the local user
Can be used to SSH to other systems the user accessed
quiz.explanation: SSH keys in a user's home directory can be used to authenticate to other systems, enabling lateral movement.

mentor_notes: Assess thoroughness of enumeration and quality of findings. Credential discovery should be careful and sanitized. 48-hour deadline is reasonable. Ethics compliance is critical when handling sensitive data.
